title: The Vault Where Flavours Are Counted

features.name: recipe-tower
features.desc-on-touch: cards stacked impossibly high, each one a recipe converted to pure numbers. you can almost taste what the ratios mean

features.name: component-shelf
features.desc-on-touch: a single shelf holding vessels of dissolved ingredients, each label a decimal point leading nowhere

features.name: notation-plaque
features.desc-on-touch: a plaque that reads only in mathematics: ∫(taste) dX. the integral has no bounds

inscription: the kitchen sent these flavours into storage as vectors and forgot how to cook with them back. now they're just dimensions

file.name: Flavor_Coefficients_v847.csv
file.note: a recipe for something no longer eaten, stored as differential equations. salt is 0.00347, regret is √2

exits.label: deeper into abstraction, where cooking becomes pure theory
exits.to: NEW: the proof-kitchen, where recipes are proven instead of eaten

exits.label: toward the archival stacks of every meal ever preserved
exits.to: NEW: the meal-records, where every plate is filed by its satisfaction index
